behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue handoff plus signature verification gate (MOCK-MISS: cross-module interaction). A pure function test can validate HMAC math, but cannot prove 'not forwarded' to queue. REAL-INFRA is not required: in-process request/response objects and a spy/fake queue are sufficient. Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML step is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the webhook handler with real signature verifier wiring and a fake queue writer. Send a request with mismatched raw body/signature and assert HTTP 401 plus zero queue write calls.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (HTTP handler and persistent queue adapter), so Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA check: no real network/OS primitive is required to detect the bug; local in-process wiring with local persistence adapter suffices. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because behavior text is about sequencing, not real HTTP transport effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to a test persistent queue implementation that records commit completion time. Invoke handler and assert queue write/commit happens before response completion callback/status 200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets decoupling between enqueue path and downstream worker completion, which is a module interaction failure (MOCK-MISS). REAL-INFRA not strictly needed: a controllable slow downstream promise/fake worker can expose waiting bugs. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because real network/OS is not required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with a downstream processor stub that blocks on a deferred promise. Send valid request and assert response is returned before deferred resolves, while enqueue still occurred.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a wall-clock SLO under concurrency. MOCK-HIDE/REAL-INFRA: fake timers or in-process-only stubs can miss event-loop contention, connection handling, and scheduling effects that appear with real HTTP/server runtime under concurrent load. Thus System is the minimum boundary. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML decisions are part of correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real Node HTTP server process and queue/worker stack used in deployment-like mode. Saturate downstream workers with slow prior events, then send concurrent new webhook requests over real HTTP and assert each acknowledgment latency is <3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness depends on state handoff between handler/idempotency check and persistent queue write path; Unit of one function can miss race/contract bugs. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with local DB/filesystem-backed test queue in-process; no external OS/network dependency beyond local persistence. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because real HTTP transport is not required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Using real dedup + queue modules with a local persistent test store, submit the same event ID twice and assert only one queue record exists and second request follows expected idempotent path.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Failure is contract mapping across modules: queue-full signal must translate to HTTP 503 + Retry-After. Unit is too low if it isolates only response formatting without real module wiring. REAL-INFRA does not require real network/subprocess; an in-process queue module configured to full state is enough. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require real transport timing/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue module to return/full-capacity error on write. Invoke handler and assert status 503 and presence/value format of Retry-After header.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets queue component plus persistence backing behavior, not just an isolated algorithm. Integration with real queue module and local persistence catches real boundary bugs (off-by-one, transaction/order issues). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real external network/subprocess; no AI dependency; no multi-agent chain. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because behavior text focuses on queue capacity logic, which is reproducible with local persistence integration.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize queue with a concrete capacity (e.g., N) against its real local persistence backend, enqueue N events successfully, then assert event N+1 is rejected with the defined capacity error path.
